commands.review_summary:
The PR adds focused mocks and a module reset in `directive-handling.model.test.ts` so `/model` directive tests avoid exercising broader `/models`, auth-label, and CLI backend discovery paths.

PR surface: Tests +91. Total +91 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a product bug; the PR body provides before/after targeted Vitest timing for the slow, order-sensitive test file, but I did not run local tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer follow-up is appropriate because this maintainer-labeled test-only PR has a current type-check failure but no line-level repair identifiable from available logs.

target.title: test(auto-reply): narrow directive model test dependencies
target.body:
## Summary

- Narrow `directive-handling.model.test.ts` so unit-level `/model` directive assertions do not exercise broad `/models`, auth-label, and CLI backend discovery paths.
- Keep `/model list` coverage focused on delegating to the models browser with the right workspace/config scope.
- Reset modules for this test file so the narrowed mocks stay effective in shared-worker shard order.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: `src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts` was exercising broad `/models`, auth-label, and CLI backend discovery paths for unit-level directive assertions, making the auto-reply dispatch shard slow and order-sensitive.

Real environment tested: Local Codex OpenClaw worktree on branch `codex/perf-auto-reply-tests` using the repo Vitest wrapper after `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` hydrated missing dependencies.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.auth.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts`
- `git diff --check -- src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts`

Evidence after fix:
- Before patch: `directive-handling.model.test.ts` failed after one 120s timeout; Vitest duration `286.14s`; wall time `7:11.31`.
- After patch: `directive-handling.model.test.ts` passed `60/60`; Vitest duration `3.58s`; wall time `1:09.46`.
- Companion seam proof passed `94/94` across `commands-models.test.ts`, `directive-handling.auth.test.ts`, and `directive-handling.model.test.ts`.
- Hotspot shard-order proof passed `284/284` across `directive-handling.model.test.ts`, `followup-runner.test.ts`, and `dispatch-from-config.test.ts`; Vitest duration `19.71s`; wall time `1:24.76`.

Observed result after fix: The slow `/model list`, status auth, and CLI runtime binding paths now stay in their owning tests, while `directive-handling.model.test.ts` remains fast even when run beside the other dispatch hotspot files.

What was not tested: I did not run the full local auto-reply reply dispatch shard or broad `check:changed` locally, per Codex-worktree guidance to avoid broad local pnpm gates.

## Related Work Search

- No exact existing issue or PR found for this test-shard cleanup.
- Nearby but not duplicate: #86229, #86635, #86547.
